what_it_does: An integration smoke test for the on-canvas composition backend. It writes a temporary Number-typed node into the nodes directory, discovers nodes via NodeRegistry, builds a Suite over a temporary FsStore, and verifies that Suite.connect rejects a Number-to-Text type mismatch, accepts a Text-to-Text connection, and that delete_node removes a node together with its incident edges.
observations: The script writes a temporary node module tmpnum.py into the source nodes/ directory and deletes it in a finally block.
It uses a custom check helper and a global PASS counter instead of unittest or pytest.
It directly calls the private Suite._load method to inspect persisted graph state.
The test creates three nodes (tmpnum, wordcount, uppercase) in a temporary graph named compose-test.
It exercises three behaviors: type-mismatch rejection, compatible connection acceptance, and node-plus-edge deletion.
Cleanup handles both the temporary store directory and the temporary node file.
standouts: Injects a synthetic Number-typed node because the existing node library is all Text/Any, so no real mismatch exists otherwise.
Relies on private Suite._load as the test oracle for persisted edges and nodes.
Uses a global PASS counter and custom assertion helper rather than a standard test framework.
conventions: Top-level docstring explains the test's purpose and the temporary-node workaround.
ROOT and sys.path manipulation to import project modules.
Temporary directory created with tempfile.mkdtemp and removed in a finally block.
Custom check function prints per-assertion status and accumulates a pass count.
Short graph and node identifiers (g, num, wc, up).
concerns: Writing a temporary file into the production nodes/ directory could leave an orphan or race with other tests if cleanup fails.
Calling Suite._load couples the test to private implementation details.
Global mutable PASS counter is not thread-safe and unusual for tests.
No standard test runner means discovery, reporting, and failure isolation are manual.
notes: A runnable acceptance check for graph wiring type safety and node deletion that temporarily adds a Number node to create a real type-mismatch scenario.
questions: Why must the temporary node be written to the source nodes/ directory instead of a separate temporary nodes directory?
What exception contract does Suite.connect guarantee for mismatched types?
Are _load's return shape and semantics considered stable enough for tests?
How does NodeRegistry behave if tmpnum.py is left behind after an interrupted run?
What is the type-compatibility rule for Any ports relative to Text or Number?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual behavior: it verifies Suite.connect accepts compatible and rejects mismatched types, and that delete_node removes nodes plus incident edges.
apparent_themes: Integration testing through real registry and suite objects
Temporary test fixtures with explicit cleanup
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to give the composition backend a quick, end-to-end behavioral check that type checking is enforced and graph mutations are edge-complete, even though the current node library lacks the diversity needed to trigger mismatches naturally.
novelty: The deliberate injection of a temporary Number-typed node to overcome a homogeneous Text/Any node library and exercise a real type-rejection path.
contribution: A concrete integration test that exercises type-rejection, compatible connection acceptance, and incident-edge deletion, and shows a pattern for temporarily extending the node registry to cover edge cases.
summary_for_embedding: Integration test for the composition backend verifying Suite.connect rejects incompatible Number-to-Text connections, accepts compatible Text-to-Text connections, and delete_node removes incident edges.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a runnable acceptance check for graph wiring type safety and node deletion cleanup using a temporary Number-typed node to create a real mismatch scenario.

symbols.does: Custom assertion helper that increments a global PASS counter and prints an ok message for each passing check.
symbols.description: The check function acts as a lightweight test assertion in lieu of a unit-test framework. It asserts the supplied condition, increments the module-level PASS counter on success, prints a formatted ok line including the label, and raises an AssertionError with the label on failure.
